---
title: ONC Hydrophone Network
---

::: {.callout-important appearance="simple" style="color: #d32f2f; border-left-color: #d32f2f; background-color: #ffebee;"}
## Improvements for this page
On the map, several hydrophones are hidden behind others, especially in hydrophone arrays.
:::


Ocean Networks Canada operates a massive spatial array of underwater acoustics monitoring across the country.
Each hydrophone has a designated **Device Code** (ex: ICLISTENHF6016) and is located at a designated **Location code** (ex: CSQH.H1).
The **Location Code** is used by the database, but they also have a **Colloquial Name** (ex: Clayoquot Slope), used by humans.
In addition, there are two **observatories** VENUS and NEPTUNE, which regroup several sites.

Most locations have a single hydrophone, while some sites have *hydrophone arrays*, which constitutes several hydrophones mounted together on a platform.
The hydrophone arrays naming convention has .H1, .H2, etc., added to the location code, to denote the position of the hydrophone within the array.

### Main locations
* **Pacific:** NEPTUNE (offshore) and VENUS (coastal) observatories.
* **Arctic:** Cambridge Bay, Gascoyne Inlet, NU.
* **Atlantic:** Conception Bay, Holyrood, NL.

# Map

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| message: false

import os, pandas as pd
from onc.onc import ONC
from dotenv import load_dotenv
import geopandas as gpd
import json

# Load ONC cable shapefile
onc_cable_shp_path = '../Instrument_and_cables_shapefiles/ONC_Cables.shp'
onc_cable = gpd.read_file(onc_cable_shp_path)
onc_cable = onc_cable.to_crs("EPSG:4326")

load_dotenv()
onc = ONC(os.getenv("ONC_API_TOKEN"))

# 1. Fetch Deployments
depl = pd.DataFrame(onc.getDeployments({"deviceCategoryCode": "HYDROPHONE"}))

# 2. Prepare Location Names
names_df = pd.read_csv('../notebooks/location_names.txt', 
                       header=None, names=['locationCode','locationName'])

# 3. Merge Names into the main deployment dataframe first
depl = depl.merge(names_df, on='locationCode', how='left')
depl['locationName'] = depl['locationName'].fillna(depl['locationCode'])
depl['siteCode'] = depl['locationCode'].str.split('.').str[0]

# 2. Group by the SITE code instead of the specific location code
df_plot = depl.groupby(['siteCode', 'lat', 'lon']).agg({
    'locationName': 'first', # Usually the name is consistent across instruments
    'depth': 'last',
    'deviceCode': lambda x: list(x),
    'end': lambda x: [pd.isna(i) for i in x],
    'locationCode': lambda x: list(x) # Keep the full codes for the popup list
}).reset_index()

# 3. Determine if the site has at least one active sensor
df_plot['any_active'] = df_plot['end'].apply(any)

# 4. Pass to OJS
cable_geojson = json.loads(onc_cable.to_json())

ojs_define(
    status_data = df_plot.to_dict(orient='list'),
    cable_data = cable_geojson
)
```


```{ojs}
//| echo: false

L = require("leaflet@1.9.4")
html`<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://unpkg.com/leaflet@1.9.4/dist/leaflet.css">`

container = html`<div style="height: 600px; border-radius: 10px; border: 1px solid #ddd;"></div>`
yield container;

{
  const map = L.map(container).setView([58, -95], 3);

  L.tileLayer('https://server.arcgisonline.com/ArcGIS/rest/services/Ocean/World_Ocean_Base/MapServer/tile/{z}/{y}/{x}', {
    attribution: 'Esri & Sources',
    maxZoom: 13
  }).addTo(map);

  // 1. Initialize Layer Groups
  const cableLayer = L.layerGroup().addTo(map);
  const inactiveLayer = L.layerGroup();
  const activeLayer = L.layerGroup().addTo(map);
  

  // 2. Add Cable Data to its group
  if (cable_data) {
    L.geoJSON(cable_data, {
      style: {
        color: 'grey',
        weight: 1,
        opacity: 0.7
      }
    }).addTo(cableLayer);
  }

  // 3. Loop and assign markers to groups
  status_data.locationCode.forEach((code, i) => {
    const isActive = status_data.any_active[i];
    const color = isActive ? "#28a745" : "#6c757d";
    const cleanName = status_data.locationName[i].replace(/Hydrophone [A-Z]/g, "Hydrophone Array");

    const deviceList = status_data.deviceCode[i].map((dev, idx) => {
      const status = status_data.end[i][idx] ? 
        '<span style="color:green;">Active</span>' : 
        '<span style="color:red;">Inactive</span>';
      return `<li><b>${dev}</b>: ${status}</li>`;
    }).join("");

    const marker = L.circleMarker([status_data.lat[i], status_data.lon[i]], {
      radius: 8,
      fillColor: color,
      color: "white",
      weight: 2,
      fillOpacity: 0.9
    })
    .bindPopup(`
      <div style="font-family: sans-serif; min-width: 200px;">
        <b style="font-size: 1.1em;">${cleanName}</b><br>
        <span style="color: #666; font-size: 0.9em;">${code}</span>
        <hr style="margin: 5px 0;">
        <p style="margin: 5px 0 0 0;"><b>Depth:</b> ${status_data.depth[i]} m</p>
        <p style="margin: 5px 0 0 0;"><b>Instruments at this site:</b></p>
        <ul style="margin: 5px 0; padding-left: 20px; font-size: 0.9em;">
          ${deviceList}
        </ul>
      </div>
    `);

    // Add marker to the appropriate group
    if (isActive) {
      marker.addTo(activeLayer);
    } else {
      marker.addTo(inactiveLayer);
    }
  });

  // 4. Create the Overlay Toggle Menu
  const overlays = {
    "<span style='color: #28a745; font-weight: bold;'>● Active Sites</span>": activeLayer,
    "<span style='color: #6c757d; font-weight: bold;'>○ Inactive Sites</span>": inactiveLayer,
    "Cables": cableLayer
  };

  // Add the control box to the top right
  L.control.layers(null, overlays, { collapsed: false }).addTo(map);
}
```


# List of all deployment sites
Here is a list of sites per **location code** and their associated location name, latitude, longitude, depth, and current status.

```{python}
#| echo: false
import pandas as pd
from IPython.display import display, HTML

# 1. Use 'depl' for the full list (not the grouped df_plot)
# We recreate the 'active_status' column based on 'end' being None
df_full_table = depl.copy()
df_full_table['active_status'] = df_full_table['end'].isna()

# 2. Select the specific columns you want
df_full_table = df_full_table[['locationCode', 'locationName', 'lat', 'lon', 'depth', 'deviceCode', 'active_status']]

# 3. Sort by Name so similar sites are grouped visually
df_full_table = df_full_table.sort_values(['locationName', 'locationCode'])

# formatting depth
df_full_table['depth'] = df_full_table['depth'].apply(lambda x: f"{x:.2f}" if pd.notnull(x) else x)

# 4. Rename for the professional header
df_full_table.columns = ['Location Code', 'Location Name', 'Latitude', 'Longitude', 'Depth (m)', 'Device Code', 'Status']

# 5. Map the boolean to your requested icons
df_full_table['Status'] = df_full_table['Status'].map({True: "🟢", False: "⚪"})

# 6. Display as a clean HTML table
display(HTML(df_full_table.to_html(index=False, classes='table table-striped table-hover')))
```
